blocks.content: Where is Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: How would I know? Isn't he in the cafeteria?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: He's not, that's why I'm asking.
Canis had a guess.
Shirone had probably gone to report about the events in Heaven to Alpheas.
He could tell them and get rid of these annoying guys, but he wasn't in the mood for that.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: What do you want with Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: I'm going to crush him. You said Shirone is the best, so if I beat him, you'll fall in line under me too.
Canis laughed at the absurdity.
Then Closer approached him with glaring eyes.

blocks.speaker: Closer
blocks.content: What's so funny? Don't you know who we are?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Do you even know who Shirone is?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: We know. He's the one who has reached the level of Immortal Function. But so what? If you think being a mage is like meditating, you're mistaken. Magic is a killing art, plain and simple.
Canis had no objection to that either.
After hanging around wise-like man like Shirone, it was refreshing to hear a bit of harsh truth.
But if Shirone was really just a kind and naive pushover, he would have been trampled on by him a long time ago.
Shirone was different. That was the key.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: I'm telling you this out of concern, but you should really avoid messing with him. You remind me of my younger self.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hahaha! So you're saying that just because you lost, I will too? Turns out you're just a loser.
Canis remained calm.
The years he lived were not beautiful enough to waste his emotions on cheap provocations.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It doesn't matter whether you win or lose. Have you ever hit rock bottom?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: I don't know what you're talking about, but I've never fallen. Unlike you.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Then give it a try. You'll find out what it's like to be crushed to the lowest depths you can imagine. Kukukuk.
Dante's face flushed red.
Back in the capital, Bashka, no one dared to look down on him.
It wasn't because this place was impressive but because they were ignorant.
From the start, he shouldn't have bothered dealing with these country bumpkins.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: That's funny. I'll take him down. And when I do, you'll be the one kneeling before me.
When Dante disappeared with his crew, Canis lost his appetite.
He couldn't understand why so many nobles lived in such a self-centered way.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: That's surprising, Canis. You just said that Shirone was the best.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I hate dealing with trouble. I don't want any attention in a place like this.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Well, I guess Shirone can handle himself just fine.
Canis pouted.
Even though he didn't know for sure, he thought that Arin, as a member of the Heaven Crew, would be a little worried.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: That's true, but… why does that sound so irresponsible?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Urm, no. I really think it'll be okay if it's Shirone.
Arin said this as she licked her fingers, then looked at the rice ball still in Canis's hand.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Are you finished with that?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: No, this….
Canis defensively pulled the rice ball closer.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Yeah, I'm done. You can have it.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Hehe, thanks.
Arin regained her composure and asked, now that her hunger had subsided.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Come to think of it, Shirone seems to have a lot of enemies.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: But he also has a lot of allies. He won't be lonely.
Canis looked up at the clear sky.
Even now, Harvest was analyzing the Book of Light and Darkness that he had brought from Heaven.
Since he inherited Arcane's legacy, he didn't feel impatient.
Isn't it okay to have this kind of leisure sometimes?
They've been running around like crazy until now.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Slowly, we can take our time.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Yeah, let's do that.
Canis looked back at Arin with a smile, then shook his head as if he couldn't stop her and patted her on the back.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Eat slowly.
Battle Simulation (1)
After lunch, it was time for the integrated advanced class.
As usual, the instructor was Ethella, and the location was the training ground with the Image Zone.
After about 10 minutes, Shirone came running out of breath.
He had gotten so used to teleporting during the break that he hadn't realized how exhausting it would be to run here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, Teacher Ethella. I'm sorry. I tried to make it on time.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: That's okay. Nade told me about it. Go ahead and get ready for class.
Dante and his group realized that meeting Shirone during lunch had been impossible from the start, leaving them with sour expressions.
If that were the case, why didn't someone just tell them instead of leading them on a wild goose chase?
Alright, everyone. It's good to see you all. I see we have some new faces this semester.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Dante, Closer, Savina, welcome to Alpheas Magic Academy. Let's all give them a round of applause.
Dante raised the corners of his mouth in amazement.
He had high expectations since it was said to be a subject taught by Romi Ethella, who was famous even at the Royal Magic Academy, but the impression she gave was beyond his expectations.
She had a gentle face, large glasses, and her hair was loosely tied.
He heard she was a monk, so he could understand that much, but her teaching style reminded him of the nanny who had taught him when he was seven.
What was even more baffling was that the students were clapping seriously, even at this childish instruction.
Let's start the lesson.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Lower-class students, focus on the four-way transformation and sequencing exercises. Upper-class students, work on mental pulse, targeting, and sequencing.
Savina whispered to Closer.

blocks.speaker: Savina
blocks.content: It seems like the graduation and advanced classes are completely different here.

blocks.speaker: Closer
blocks.content: Yeah. I was looking forward to the practical training session, but it's just as boring.
The only thing Savina was looking forward to when she transferred was the class taught by Ethella, who had been given the title of Triple.
The title 'Triple' was given to someone who had achieved level 6 or higher in three different fields, and Ethella had earned this title in magic, martial arts, and spiritual discipline (Spirit Zone).
She was a rising star in the education world.
But once the class started, Savina found that not only was Ethella's tone childlike, but the curriculum was just as dull, focusing on basic training.
Savina raised her hand and asked a question.

blocks.speaker: Savina
blocks.content: Teacher, aren't we going to do other training? I'd like to learn the Sniper Mode.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: That's a specialized subject, so it's not taught in the advanced class.

blocks.speaker: Savina
blocks.content: But I'm sure Ms. Ethella can teach me well. To be honest, I transferred here just to take this one class.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: The advanced class is about finding your aptitude. Even Sniper Mode ultimately stems from the transformation of the four-way system.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: You won't be too late in learning applied techniques once you've thoroughly mastered the basics.
Umm, is there no way you could teach it to me privately?

blocks.speaker: Savina
blocks.content: If I could say I learned from Professor Ethella, I could brag to my friends.
It seemed that, in the capital region, the prestige of one's teacher was a significant point of pride.
While that might be the case in Bashka, it wasn't something Ethella could address.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: I would like to, but it wouldn't be fair to others.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: If you're really eager to learn Sniper Mode, then work hard to improve your skills and join the graduation class as soon as possible.
A loud voice was heard at the entrance to the training ground.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: That's not necessarily the case. Being too strict about the fundamentals can sometimes hinder progress.
Olivia walked in, followed by all the teachers from the advanced classes like attendants.
Along with Ethella, Thad and Sienna, who are called the three pillars of Alpheas Magic Academy, were also seen.
The two exchanged glances with Ethella, shrugging as if to say, 'Can you believe this?'
It was only possible because they were outside Olivia's line of sight.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Hello, Principal.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: From what I observed at the entrance, a student here is eager to learn applied techniques.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: If a student's passion lies in learning, shouldn't a teacher be willing to teach?

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: But according to the rules...

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Aah, I'm aware of that. However, as time passes, rules can change.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Having someone as renowned as Zoner as a teacher and only having them teach the basics seems quite inefficient to me.
Since Ethella was such a great Zoner, Alpheus had deliberately asked her to teach the kids the basics in the advanced class.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Each of these students has a unique personality.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Trying to mold them all under the same class structure is futile.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: If there's room for improvement, we need to actively push for it.
Although Thad and Sienna are never one to avoid debate, they are keeping their mouths tightly shut right now.
The rank of a certified 2nd-rank archmage was that significant.
The criteria for classifying the ranks of mages were unified worldwide.
It was called the Line, and it was a huge human resource network system that managed all mages.
There were three main Lines in the world, with the Red Line, managed by the Magic Association, being the broadest channel.
All mages, classified from 1st to 10th class, whether certified or not, were part of the Red Line.
Teachers within the Red Line system knew how incredibly difficult it was to reach Olivia's level.
Since there are usually less than five certified 1st-rank mages in the kingdom, even a certified 2nd-rank mage is considered the pinnacle of the Red Line.
Thus, no matter how eccentric her philosophy might be, there was no room to argue against someone who had risen to the rank of a certified 2nd-rank mage.
Ethella spoke up.
As a Zoner, she was also of Red Line, but on the other hand, as a monk with no connection to the mages network, she had more freedom to speak than the other teachers.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Students in the advanced class still lack a deep understanding of magic.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: I believe it's important not just to have a personality that stands out from others, but to find a unique individuality that no one else can imitate.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: That's why we emphasize the basics so much.
The teachers' faces turned pale.
On the other hand, Thad and Sienna secretly raised their thumbs.
Ethella might have a gentle appearance, but her inner strength was undeniable.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Is that what you think? Fine.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Then how about we verify how solid the basics are of the students Teacher Sienna have taught using the Lifting Evaluation Method?
Ethella had heard of the Lifting Evaluation Method at academic conferences.
It was often said to be more objective than other evaluation methods and was an effective test for assessing the overall level of a Spirit Zone.
Shirone and his group felt their hearts race as they watched the teachers' fight.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whew, not just Teacher Ethella, but the Principal isn't one to be taken lightly either.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's right. If this continues, something will really go wrong, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No way. Even Teacher Sienna can't say a word.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Very well. I'm not boasting, but I'm confident that the basics of the students I've taught can hold up against any other academy.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: In that case, let's begin.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Has anyone in Class Four ever done Spirit Zone Lifting training?
The only ones who raised their hands were Dante's group.
Alpheas had rejected it due to a lack of proven effectiveness, but the Royal Magic Academy had already formally adopted it a year ago.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: I see. Then, since everyone is probably curious about the transfer student's capabilities, would you mind giving us a demonstration, Dante?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Yes. It is my honor, Principal.
As Dante walked to the center of the Image Zone, Olivia instructed the teacher to set the target's sensitivity to the maximum level.
Ethella then explained the Lifting test to the students.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: The Lifting Test is designed to evaluate the overall functionality of the Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: It involves holding a highly reactive target within the Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Since the target bounces in an unspecified direction depending on the change in density, specific gravity, and shape of Spirit Zone, the longer it is held, the more functional and effective one's Spirit Zone is.
Dante positioned himself at the center of the Image Zone.
With a snap of his fingers, a spherical target materialized above his head.
